From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Magazine of the western United States

Not to be confused with [Overland (magazine)](/wiki/Overland_(magazine) "Overland (magazine)").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e9/The_Overland_Monthly_1869.jpg/250px-The_Overland_Monthly_1869.jpg)](/wiki/File:The_Overland_Monthly_1869.jpg)

*The Overland Monthly*, 1869

The ***Overland Monthly*** was a monthly [literary](/wiki/Literary_magazine "Literary magazine") and cultural magazine, based in [California](/wiki/California "California"), United States. It was founded in 1868 and published between the second half of the 19th century and the first half of the 20th century.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Overland_Monthly&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/11/BretHarte1868.JPG/250px-BretHarte1868.JPG)](/wiki/File:BretHarte1868.JPG)

[Bret Harte](/wiki/Bret_Harte "Bret Harte"), 1868

The *Overland Monthly* was founded in 1868[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) by Anton Roman, a [Bavarian](/wiki/Bavaria "Bavaria")-born bookseller who moved to [California](/wiki/California "California") during the [Gold Rush](/wiki/California_Gold_Rush "California Gold Rush"). He had recently published the poems of [Charles Warren Stoddard](/wiki/Charles_Warren_Stoddard "Charles Warren Stoddard") and a collection of verse by California writers called *Outcroppings*.[[3]](#cite_note-3) The magazine's first issue was published in July 1868, edited by [Bret Harte](/wiki/Bret_Harte "Bret Harte") in [San Francisco](/wiki/San_Francisco "San Francisco"), and continued until late 1875. Roman, who hoped his magazine would "help the material development of this Coast", was originally concerned that Harte would "lean too much toward the purely literary". Harte, who had been editor of both *[The Golden Era](/wiki/The_Golden_Era "The Golden Era")* and *The Californian*,[[4]](#cite_note-4) was in turn skeptical at first that there would be enough quality content provided from local authors.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The first issue included contributions from the "Golden State Trinity": Harte, Stoddard, and [Ina Coolbrith](/wiki/Ina_Coolbrith "Ina Coolbrith").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Despite the positive response from critics and the magazine's profitability, publisher Anton Roman sold the *Overland Monthly* in June 1869 for $7,500 to John Carmany.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Harte immediately offered the new owner a list of demands, including a raise to $200 a month and a guarantee of his complete editorial control of each issue.[[8]](#cite_note-Tarnoff173-8) Carmany agreed to his terms, and Harte was able to leave his job at the [San Francisco Mint](/wiki/San_Francisco_Mint "San Francisco Mint") to devote his full attention to the *Overland Monthly*.[[9]](#cite_note-Scharnhorst51-9) The publication continued to thrive in this period; [Mark Twain](/wiki/Mark_Twain "Mark Twain") reported that he had "heard it handsomely praised by some of the most ponderous of America's literary chiefs."[[8]](#cite_note-Tarnoff173-8)

In the September 1870 issue, Harte published what became his most well-known work, "Plain Language from Truthful James", later known as "[The Heathen Chinee](/wiki/The_Heathen_Chinee "The Heathen Chinee")".[[9]](#cite_note-Scharnhorst51-9) That year, with his popularity soaring, Harte considered a professorship at the [University of California, Berkeley](/wiki/University_of_California,_Berkeley "University of California, Berkeley") or an offer to purchase the *Overland Monthly*, but declined both. Instead, he left California and traveled east to seek broader literary fame.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

The original publishers, in 1880, started [*The Californian*](/wiki/The_Californian_(1880s_magazine) "The Californian (1880s magazine)"), which became *The Californian and Overland Monthly* in October 1882. In January 1883, the effort reverted to *The Overland Monthly* (starting again with Volume I, number 1).[[11]](#cite_note-11) The 1884 volume contained a commitment to present content "free of advertising taint," explaining that no article would appear that was not "in good faith what it appears to be."[[12]](#cite_note-12) Milicent Shinn wrote about her years as editor from 1883 to 1894.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

The Overland Monthly was based in San Francisco until at least 1921.[[14]](#cite_note-14) In 1923 the magazine merged with *[Out West](/wiki/The_Land_of_Sunshine "The Land of Sunshine")* to become *Overland Monthly and the Out West magazine*, and ended publication in July 1935.

## Contributors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Overland_Monthly&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Contributors")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/ce/MuttandJeffAdvertisement.jpg/250px-MuttandJeffAdvertisement.jpg)](/wiki/File:MuttandJeffAdvertisement.jpg)

SF Chronicle Mutt and Jeff advertisement in the *Overland*, 1916

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/32/OverlandMonthlyJanuary1919.JPG/250px-OverlandMonthlyJanuary1919.JPG)](/wiki/File:OverlandMonthlyJanuary1919.JPG)

*Overland Monthly*, January 1919

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4c/Overland_Monthly_advertisement_in_The_Black_Cat_of_April_1912.jpg/250px-Overland_Monthly_advertisement_in_The_Black_Cat_of_April_1912.jpg)](/wiki/File:Overland_Monthly_advertisement_in_The_Black_Cat_of_April_1912.jpg)

Overland Monthly advertisement in [The Black Cat](/wiki/The_Black_Cat_(US_magazine) "The Black Cat (US magazine)") of April 1912

Noted writers, editors, and artists associated with the magazine included:

* [Robert Ingersoll Aitken](/wiki/Robert_Ingersoll_Aitken "Robert Ingersoll Aitken")
* [Ambrose Bierce](/wiki/Ambrose_Bierce "Ambrose Bierce")
* [Noah Brooks](/wiki/Noah_Brooks "Noah Brooks")
* [Alice Cary](/wiki/Alice_Cary "Alice Cary")
* [Willa Cather](/wiki/Willa_Cather "Willa Cather")
* [Charles W. Chesnutt](/wiki/Charles_W._Chesnutt "Charles W. Chesnutt")
* [Frona Eunice Wait Colburn](/wiki/Frona_Eunice_Wait_Colburn "Frona Eunice Wait Colburn")
* [Bret Harte](/wiki/Bret_Harte "Bret Harte")
* [Ina Coolbrith](/wiki/Ina_Coolbrith "Ina Coolbrith")
* [Maynard Dixon](/wiki/Maynard_Dixon "Maynard Dixon")
* [Edgar Fawcett](/wiki/Edgar_Fawcett "Edgar Fawcett")
* [Henry George](/wiki/Henry_George "Henry George")
* [John Brayshaw Kaye](/wiki/John_Brayshaw_Kaye "John Brayshaw Kaye")
* [Charmian Kittredge](/wiki/Charmian_London "Charmian London")
* [Netta Eames](/wiki/Netta_Eames "Netta Eames")
* [Clarence King](/wiki/Clarence_King "Clarence King")
* [Kinahan Cornwallis](/wiki/Kinahan_Cornwallis "Kinahan Cornwallis")
* [Jack London](/wiki/Jack_London "Jack London")
* [Lannie Haynes Martin](/wiki/Lannie_Haynes_Martin "Lannie Haynes Martin")
* [Josephine Clifford McCracken](/wiki/Josephine_Clifford_McCracken "Josephine Clifford McCracken")
* [Joaquin Miller](/wiki/Joaquin_Miller "Joaquin Miller")
* [John Muir](/wiki/John_Muir "John Muir")
* [Hugo Wilhelm Arthur Nahl](/wiki/Hugo_Wilhelm_Arthur_Nahl "Hugo Wilhelm Arthur Nahl")
* [Lola Ridge](/wiki/Lola_Ridge "Lola Ridge")
* [Charles Taze Russell](/wiki/Charles_Taze_Russell "Charles Taze Russell")
* [Stephen Powers](/wiki/Stephen_Powers "Stephen Powers")
* [William Saroyan](/wiki/William_Saroyan "William Saroyan")
* [Herman George Scheffauer](/wiki/Herman_George_Scheffauer "Herman George Scheffauer")
* [Charles Howard Shinn](/wiki/Charles_Howard_Shinn "Charles Howard Shinn")
* [Milicent Shinn](/wiki/Milicent_Shinn "Milicent Shinn")
* [Edward Rowland Sill](/wiki/Edward_Rowland_Sill "Edward Rowland Sill")
* [Clark Ashton Smith](/wiki/Clark_Ashton_Smith "Clark Ashton Smith")
* [Josephine Spencer](/wiki/Josephine_Spencer "Josephine Spencer")
* [Charles Warren Stoddard](/wiki/Charles_Warren_Stoddard "Charles Warren Stoddard")[[15]](#cite_note-15)
* [Augustus Gabriel de Vivier Tassin](/wiki/Augustus_Gabriel_de_Vivier_Tassin "Augustus Gabriel de Vivier Tassin")
* [Douglas Tilden](/wiki/Douglas_Tilden "Douglas Tilden")
* [Mark Twain](/wiki/Mark_Twain "Mark Twain")
* [Frances Fuller Victor](/wiki/Frances_Fuller_Victor "Frances Fuller Victor")
* [Laura Lyon White](/wiki/Laura_Lyon_White "Laura Lyon White")
* [Joseph Widney](/wiki/Joseph_Widney "Joseph Widney")

Editors include:

* [Milicent Shinn](/wiki/Milicent_Shinn "Milicent Shinn") 1883-1894 [[16]](#cite_note-16)
* [Rounsevelle Wildman](/wiki/Rounsevelle_Wildman "Rounsevelle Wildman"), editor 1894-1897[[17]](#cite_note-17)
* [James Howard Bridge](/wiki/James_Howard_Bridge "James Howard Bridge"), editor 1897-1900
